51: function detectEvidenceType(mimeType: string): EvidenceType {
52:   if (mimeType.startsWith('image/')) return 'screenshot';

547: export const toolNames = definitions.map(d => d.name);
548: 
549: export async function handle(
550:   name: string,
551:   args: Record<string, unknown>,
552:   deps: ToolDependencies
553: ): Promise<ToolResponse> {
554:   const { stateManager, wsHandler, sessionId } = deps;
555: 
556:   // Validate org ownership before any feature operations
557:   const orgValidationError = await validateSessionOrgAccess(stateManager, sessionId, getCurrentOrgId());
558:   if (orgValidationError) {
559:     return orgValidationError;
560:   }
561: 
562:   switch (name) {
563:     case 'update_features': {
564:       // Session MUST have taskId to create features
565:       const session = await stateManager.getSession(sessionId);
566:       if (!session) {
567:         return errorResponse('session_not_found', `Session ${sessionId} not found`);
568:       }
569: 
570:       if (!session.epicId) {
571:         // Fetch available epics to include in error context
572:         const orgId = getCurrentOrgId();
573:         let availableEpics: Array<{ id: string; title: string }> = [];
574:         if (orgId) {
575:           try {
576:             const result = await stateManager.listEpicSummariesPaginated(orgId, { limit: 5 });
577:             availableEpics = result.items.map(e => ({ id: e.id, title: e.title }));
578:           } catch {
579:             // Ignore - just won't include context
580:           }
581:         }
582: 
583:         return workflowErrorResponse(
584:           'no_epic_linked',
585:           'Session must be linked to an Epic before creating features.',
586:           {
587:             tool: 'session_init',
588:             description: 'Initialize session with an Epic',
589:             example_params: availableEpics.length > 0
590:               ? { epic_id: availableEpics[0].id }
591:               : { epic_id: 'EPIC-ID' },
592:           },
593:           {
594:             hint: 'Features live in Epic → Task hierarchy. Link your session to an Epic first.',
595:             workflow_state: 'needs_epic',
596:             ...(availableEpics.length > 0 ? { available_epics: availableEpics } : {}),
597:           }
598:         );
599:       }
600: 
601:       if (!session.taskId) {
602:         // Fetch available tasks from the linked epic
603:         let availableTasks: Array<{ id: string; title: string }> = [];
604:         try {
605:           const tasks = await stateManager.listTasks(session.epicId);
606:           availableTasks = tasks
607:             .filter(t => t.status === 'ready')
608:             .slice(0, 5)
609:             .map(t => ({ id: t.id, title: t.title }));
610:         } catch {
611:           // Ignore - just won't include context
612:         }
613: 
614:         return workflowErrorResponse(
615:           'no_task_set',
616:           'Session must have a claimed task before creating features.',
617:           {
618:             tool: 'claim_task',
619:             description: 'Claim an available task from the Epic',
620:             example_params: availableTasks.length > 0
621:               ? { task_id: availableTasks[0].id }
